what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record that maps an attachment identifier to an image target in the design-source channel and records when it was added.
observations: The file consists entirely of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It declares an attachment id `att-59fadc60` for channel `design-source`.
The `target` is a URI of the form `image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-30`.
standouts: Use of a custom `image://` URI scheme to address the target asset.
Attachment typed as `images` rather than a generic file or document.
The file is a metadata stub with no embedded image data or descriptive caption.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter delimited by `---` for structured metadata.
Records an explicit UTC timestamp with timezone offset.
References an external asset by URI instead of inlining it.
concerns: The body is empty, so there is no human-readable description of the attachment.
No `added_by`, author, or source message context is provided.
No file size, dimensions, MIME type, or checksum is recorded.
The meaning of `p-30` and the exact image resource are not defined inside this file.
notes: A minimal, timestamped metadata stub that points an attachment ID to an image asset in the design-source channel.
questions: What is the pitch or content represented by `p-30`?
Who added this attachment and in which message or conversation?
Is the `image://` URI resolvable within the project's storage system?
Are there other attachments or versions associated with the same target?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an attachment ledger entry for a channel, and the file does exactly that: it records a typed link to an external image target with a timestamp.
apparent_themes: Decoupled storage of binary assets via URI references
Minimal metadata stubs over embedded content
intention_signals: The file appears to be registering an image attachment for a recent design pitch so that the asset can be referenced, retrieved, and audited by its attachment id.
novelty: The use of an `image://` URI scheme and an `images` attachment type in otherwise conventional YAML frontmatter is the most distinctive feature.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed pointer from a stable attachment id to a channel-scoped image asset, supporting retrieval and provenance tracking without embedding the media.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment record with id `att-59fadc60` linking the `design-source` channel to the image target `image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-30` and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image attachment for a design-source pitch so it can be referenced by a stable attachment id.
